observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Assessor to exert effort for the speedy titling of land and acquire Transfer Certificate of Title in the name of the LGU in order to protect the interest of the LGU for any adverse claims by third parties and in compliance with the afore-cited laws, rules and regulations.

We also recommended that Management establish the actual and legal ownership of the LGU to the aforesaid parcels of lot before proceeding to any lawful actions against occupants of the lots and/or those individuals with claims to the same and refrain from financing any development or improvement to the land with legal issues/encumbrances and questionable ownership to avoid wastage of government funds.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to prepare and submit the required reports and records relative to Local Roads Asset Management;
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to reconcile their records to arrive at the correct listings of the road networks; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide the required disclosures in the Notes to the Financial Statements relative to Road Network account.

We recommended that Management direct the Supply Officer to initiate the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with established guidelines. Furthermore, we recommend strict adherence to the procedures outlined in the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties when determining the appraisal values of PPE for disposal, as well as in selecting the appropriate mode of disposal to ensure transparency, efficiency, and proper asset management.

We recommended that Management direct the co-chairman, with the assistance of the Municipal Accountant, to supervise the submission and furnishing of copies of the approved SEF budget and any SEF supplemental budgets to the LGU offices concerned, as well as the quarterly and annual utilization reports of the SEF budget to the offices and oversight agencies concerned within the prescribed timelines. This will not only enhance the transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of SEF by the Municipal Government but will also reinforce the efforts of the national government in identifying the immediate needs of public schools.

We recommended that Management instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to intensify tax collection strategies on taxes due and collectible to maximize collection of real property taxes and other taxes due the LGU by sending notice of delinquency to taxpayers and forming committee to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes.

We further recommended that the LGU avail the legal remedies provided by law in the collection of delinquent taxes to satisfy taxes, penalties and costs.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to:
Create a multi-year GAD Agenda as basis for the annual GAD Plan and Budget by using the templates provided in the PCW Memorandum Circular; and
Conduct a GAD Orientation focusing in training concerned LGU officials and/or employees on the importance of the GAD Agenda and its legal basis.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Engineer to submit the approved As-Built plans and Contractor’s Affidavit on payment of laborers and materials for the completed projects for technical evaluation and inspection by the COA Regional Technical Services; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to hold the processing of any claim without the required complete supporting documents.

Uncollected stall rentals totaling ₱243,125.00 were not acccnted and recorded in the books as of December 31, 2022 resulting in the understatement of both Accounts Receivable and income access which is not in accordance with Paragraph of IPSAS No. 9. Likewise, the inefficient collection of revenue deprived the LGU from generating additional funds which could have been used to meet its obligations or implement other beneficial programs, projects and activities (PPA).
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to adhere to accrual method of accounting by properly recognizing uncollected stall rentals pursuant to Paragraph 19 IPSAS 9.
Further, we recommended that Management instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to continue maximizing their efforts in collecting delinquent rental fees to generate additional income for economic development of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
1.10. We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit to the
Municipal Treasurer, on or before the 31st day of December each year,
an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties
have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of each property;
b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant
the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount
due and collectible for the year;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV at the beginning of
the year to record as debit the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable
accounts and as credit the Deferred RPT Income/ Deferred SET
Income accounts upon receipt of the duly certified list from the
Treasurer’s Office showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due
and collectible;
d. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to exert
efforts to locate the documents to establish the details of the recorded
RPT Receivables amounting to ₱9,308,895.70; and
e. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to monitor and reconcile the
recorded RPT and SET Receivable accounts at the end of each month
to ensure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

2.8. We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
reclassify the amount of ₱2,554,951.53 representing cash shortage of the
former Municipal Treasurer to the Due from Officers and Employees account.

3.6. We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
• Observe the perpetual inventory system in recording purchases;

• Prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for each item of
supplies and materials or inventory account; and

• Adhere to the guidelines in accounting and valuation of inventories
to have an accurate presentation of accounts in the financial
statements.

b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
• Maintain Stock Cards for each item of supplies and materials or
inventory account;
• Consolidate the RIS issued and prepare SSMI which shall serve as
the basis of the Municipal Accountant in recording the issued
supplies as expense; and
• Issue ICS on the issuance of items/property with serviceable life of
more than one year but small enough to be considered as PPE.

c. Create an Inventory Committee and instruct it to conduct physical
inventory of supplies semestrally and to prepare and submit the RPCI
to the Audit Team; and
d. Provide stockroom with ample space for the use of the Municipal
Treasurer in storing the supplies and materials purchased including
equipment for issuance to the various offices/departments.

4.19. We recommended that Management instruct:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to:
• Strictly comply with the required physical count of all properties at
least once a year using the proper format and submit a copy of the
said report to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of every
year; and
• Prepare and maintain complete property cards.

b. The Municipal Accountant to:
• Prepare and maintain complete PPELC for each group of PPEs;
and
• Make detailed evaluation and tracing of the noted unreconciled
beginning balances amounting to ₱2,582,141.01 to reflect the
correct balances of these accounts in the financial statements.

c. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, as
well as other concerned officials, to review and study the guidelines and
procedures of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and initiate compliance to
the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

5.18. We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee to
facilitate the proper conduct of annual inventory of local roads of the LGU
and prepare the prescribed RPCLRN due for submission to the Audit Team
on or before the 31st day of January of the ensuing year.

5.19. We also recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain a Road Map;
b. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to provide complete description and
cost segregation of road components for road projects;
c. Require the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Engineer and Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the inventory of local roads with the recorded
road networks in the books of accounts;
d. Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to
prepare and maintain the local road network Property Card and
Ledger Card, respectively; and
e. Require the Municipal Accountant to provide proper and complete
disclosures in the NTFS.

6.17. We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the assigned
inventory committee, to include land owned by the LGU in the physical
count of PPE and submit the report thereon to the Audit Team;
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and keep updated
RPLC;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to exert utmost effort to determine
the details of the unaccounted amount recorded in the Land account
amounting to ₱38,494.09; and
d. Require the Municipal Assessor to coordinate closely with the
government office concerned for the release of TCTs of various lots
owned by the LGU.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
• Restate the affected accounts for all ensuing financial statement, if there is any; and
• Ensure disclosure of relevant information in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the LGU.
We further recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
• Submit the supporting documents on the recorded adjustments for further evaluation; and
• Ensure that all adjustments are properly documented to achieve faithful representation of all transactions.

We reiterated the previous years’ audit recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:
1.	Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December 31 of every year. The actual account may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is necessary in order to know the physical existence and condition  of the PPE, and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets.  Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments and to pin point responsibility/accountability for any missing PPE;
2.	Maintain/update the PPE Ledger Cards, Property Cards, Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PARs), and placing Property Tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control,  monitoring and facilitate identification of the PPE; and
3.	Make use of Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) for semi-expendable items to avoid loss as these are usually small items.

Require the Accountant to exert all effort to locate/retrieve the supporting documents and ledgers of the dormant receivable and conduct in-depth analysis of these accounts so that necessary adjustments can be identified and recorded in books; and
In case all possible remedies fail, comply with the requirements in requesting for write-off of the dormant accounts as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:
1.	Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December 31 of every year. The actual account may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the presence/physical existence of the fixed assets and their conditions, and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets.  Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments and to pin point responsibility/accountability for missing PPE’s.
2.	Maintain/update the subsidiary Ledgers, PPE Ledger Cards, Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR’s), and placing Property Tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPE’s and for facility in identifying them.
3.	For control and monitoring make use of Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) for semi-expendable items to avoid loss as these are usually small items.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-Municipal Assessor  exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the Municipal Government of Labrador in compliance with the provisions of Section 39 of PD 1445 and Section 449 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual Volume I.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of tax payers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis of the latter in setting-up the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of each year.

We have recommended the Local Chief Executive instruct the Accountant   to account and analyze the dormant accounts, to determine their validity and recommend for their proper disposition pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We reiterated our previous year’s  recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee to justify the noted lapses, and comply with the law, rules and regulations as required by IRR of RA 9184.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the  Bids and Awards Committee prepare and submit to the Head of the Procuring Entity the Procurement and Monitoring Report and submit the same  to the GPPB pursuant to IRR of RA 9184.
We further recommended that the BAC furnish the Audit Team copy of the said report in order for us to verify/validate the status of the procurement process   and to be used as basis for our contract review on the procured goods and infrastructure projects.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation  that management consolidate the PPMP into an APP as an effective planning tool in the procurement of goods and services aimed at reducing procurement costs through competitive bidding.
We also recommended that management designate a dedicated officer-in charge of procuring activities to ensure that effective planning and implementation of procurement activities are achieved.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to revert any unutilized amount of LDRRMF after five years to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the accounting and reporting guidelines for the LDRRMF which shall be used for other social services to reflect valid and accurate balances as of December 31, 2017.

We recommended that the LDRRMO incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the detailed planned projects/program/activities chargeable to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from payments made in accordance with the Revised Withholding Tax Table.
We further recommended that the BAC Secretariat Head  submit the above-mentioned documents for post audit to establish the validity of the claims thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-Municipal Engineer to adhere strictly to the above-mentioned rules and regulations in order to ensure  quality of the works done by the contractor and also,  there is an assurance that all  projects  were implemented in   accordance with the plans and specifications.

We   recommended that the BAC Chairman and the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184 by requiring all suppliers/contractors to submit their current and updated Certificate of PhilGEPS Registration as  indicated in the RFQ or RFP at what stage of the procurement  process these requirements must be submitted either on the submission of offer/s, evaluation of offer/s, before the issuance of Notice of Award or prior to payment thereof.
We recommended that the current Local School Board revisit the provisions of the Section 98(b)  and reorganize the composition of the Municipal School Board.

We  recommended that management continue to appropriate one percent of its Internal Revenue Allotment for the Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC) and ensure proper implementation  of programs and activities that would address  and focus towards child protection in order to prevent juvenile delinquency.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive   instruct the OIC-Municipal Engineer and other officials concerned to disclose all transactions involving PPAs for transparency and accountability in order to avoid suspensions/disallowances in audit.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive  strictly enforce the segregation of wastes at  source and to provide for a sanitary landfill for the disposal of collected wastes in accordance with the 10–year Municipal Solid Waste Management (MSWM) Plan. Continue to oblige all the barangays for their participation in the information dissemination.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipality continue to appropriate at least 5%  of the total annual appropriations and to incorporate only GAD related programs and activities in the GAD Plan and Budget to address issues and concerns affecting the welfare and full development of women in order to fully achieve the GAD commitment on women’s empowerment and gender equality.
We recommended further that management stop  charging  expenses not in accordance with the PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01, The GAD Planning and Budgeting Guidelines.  Unrelated disbursements should be charged to the regular Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) of the LGU.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive  instruct the Municipal Treasurer to adopt the following measures:
•  Update and post notice of delinquency for property taxes at the main entrance of the municipal building and in publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay where these properties located.
• Continue to send Notices of Delinquency with the assistance from the concerned barangays  and form a committee to conduct an information  dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes.
• If warranted, enforced the provisions of Sections 258 and 260 of the Local Government Code of 1991 in the collections of delinquent taxes.
